result.summary: Found 4 visual issues on the BetRivers Pennsylvania casino page, primarily related to content truncation and clipped UI elements on the right side of the screen.

issues.description: Game cards in the right column are visually cut off. Game titles like 'Magni Pho...', 'Munc Mi...', and 'Hyperc...' are partially visible, indicating a horizontal overflow or clipping issue with the grid layout.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the game card grid uses proper padding/margin so cards are either fully visible or clearly indicate they are scrollable. Use CSS overflow handling or implement a horizontal scroll with visual affordance (arrows, fade effect).

issues.description: The top navigation category tab 'Hot Ga...' is cut off at the right edge of the screen, truncating the label without any scroll indicator or affordance to suggest more tabs exist.
issues.suggestion: Add a visual fade or arrow indicator on the right edge of the tab bar to signal horizontal scrollability. Ensure at least the partial tab is clearly recognizable as interactive and scrollable.

issues.description: Game title text on partially visible right-column cards is unreadable due to clipping (e.g., 'Magni Pho', 'Munc Mi'), which could confuse users about what game is being shown.
issues.suggestion: Either show full card titles with ellipsis truncation in a readable area, or ensure the card layout scrolls properly so full content is accessible.

issues.description: The bottom navigation bar icons and labels appear slightly cramped with very little spacing between items (Casino, Live Casino, Sports, Poker, Promos, Support), which may cause tap target issues on smaller devices.
issues.suggestion: Increase horizontal spacing or reduce the number of visible bottom nav items, using a 'More' option if necessary, to meet minimum touch target size guidelines (44x44px recommended).
